expected_deltas.content: Signing key rotation cycle was shortened from 7 days to 24 hours
expected_deltas.source_span: all session tokens are issued with a shortened signing key rotation cycle of 24 hours, down from the previous 7-day cycle

expected_deltas.content: The shortened 24-hour rotation cycle forces simultaneous re-authentication across all devices when a rotation boundary is crossed
expected_deltas.source_span: because signing keys now rotate every 24 hours, any session token issued before a rotation boundary becomes invalid once the next rotation occurs, forcing the user to reauthenticate on all previously logged-in devices simultaneously
expected_deltas.note: The cause (24h rotation) is introduced in paragraph 1; the effect (forced simultaneous re-auth) only appears in paragraph 4, after two intervening paragraphs about client config and audit logging. Tests whether extraction links across non-adjacent content instead of only catching same-paragraph relationships.

expected_deltas.content: The forced re-authentication behavior has caused a measurable increase in support tickets about unexpected logouts
expected_deltas.source_span: This has already resulted in a measurable increase in support tickets related to unexpected logouts
expected_deltas.note: A second causal hop from the same root cause — tests whether the pipeline can trace a chain two links deep, not just recover isolated pairwise relationships.
